Title: Zahid: Anwar's ties could help free Malaysians held after flotilla interception
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia's diplomatic ties with friendly nations could pave the way for efforts to secure the release of 10 Malaysians detained after vessels in the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 mission were intercepted in international waters en route to Gaza.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's strong international relations could help facilitate negotiations.
"Certainly, the good relations enjoyed by the prime minister may allow diplomatic efforts to be undertaken, not only to facilitate their journey to deliver aid, but also to secure their release once the mission is completed.
"God willing, I will ask the Foreign Minister to make efforts and play his role," he told reporters.
Zahid said Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan should play an active role in assisting the detained Malaysian delegates.
He was speaking after officiating and witnessing the memorandum of understanding (MoU) exchange ceremony between MyDigital ID Sdn Bhd and its strategic partners here today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 11 squatter areas in Sabah's Kapayan flagged as fire-prone
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Eleven squatter settlements at high risk of fire have been identified in Kapayan, said the Assistant Minister of Local Government and Housing.
Based on records from the Penampang Municipal Council (MPP), Datuk Maijol Mahap said the settlements are located on river reserves, along drains and on hilly terrain.
"Most of these areas have high population density, closely packed and unplanned housing layouts, and use building materials that are highly flammable.
"In addition, narrow and limited access routes can hinder the smooth operation of rescue teams and firefighters in the event of a fire," he said.
He was responding to a question from (Warisan–Kapayan) Chin Tek Ming during the state legislative assembly on the number of squatter settlements at high risk of fire in the area.
Maijol added that the state government, through the relevant ministry, would ensure the safety of residents.
On April 19, a
pre-dawn fire destroyed
about 1,000 homes at Kampung Bahagia in Sandakan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Michael Chong calls for revival of preventive laws to combat loan shark abuses
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MCA Public Services and Complaints Department head Datuk Seri Michael Chong has proposed reinstating relevant preventive laws to more effectively combat increasingly rampant illegal lending operations.
He said some borrowers continue to be harassed for additional interest even after fully repaying their loans.
"Victims have faced intimidation tactics such as red paint being splashed on their properties, threats of arson and the online dissemination of personal information, all of which severely affect their safety and daily lives," he said in a statement today.
He said the proposal was made during a courtesy visit to the Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) by the MCA Public Services and Complaints Department and the Malaysia–China Friendship and Mutual Assistance Association today.
The delegation included association president Ee Kah Fuk, deputy president Datuk Seri Haw Swee Tit, as well as MCA Complaints Department legal advisers Tan Kai Yong and Teoh Wei Loong.
The group was received by CCID director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa.
Chong also submitted numerous complaint cases received by the department to Rusdi.
He said the meeting also discussed invoking Section 116D of the Criminal Procedure Code to freeze or restrict transactions from bank or financial accounts involved in such crimes, to strengthen enforcement efforts.
Meanwhile, Ee raised concerns over a rising trend of fraud cases involving cross-border payment platforms such as Alipay and WeChat Pay.
"These cases often involve international elements, with victims frequently foreign nationals and suspects possibly originating from Malaysia or other countries, making enforcement and investigations more challenging," he said.
He cited the case of a Chinese student in Malaysia who, lacking Malaysian currency to pay tuition fees, entrusted a local individual to assist with the payment.
"After transferring renminbi to the individual's designated Alipay account, the person disappeared without fulfilling the promise, causing financial loss to the victim."
The meeting also discussed a recent staged accident scam.
"If left unchecked, such activities could lead to real traffic accidents, endangering innocent road users," Chong said, urging police to expedite investigations and bring the perpetrators to justice.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ekuinas elevates art economy push
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Ekuiti Nasional Bhd's (Ekuinas) Iltizam Seni exhibition returned this year with a sharper ambition to position art not merely as cultural expression but as a catalyst for economic value, entrepreneurship and long-term industry growth in Malaysia.
Now in its third cycle, Iltizam Seni, the arts pillar under Ekuinas' broader social impact platform Iltizam, was conceived to create a more deliberate pathway for emerging Bumiputera artists to move beyond passion and into sustainable, income-generating careers.
Speaking at the graduation cum exhibition, Ekuinas chief executive officer Aliff Omar Mohammed Omar said the initiative reflects a broader belief that artistic talent, when paired with the right structure and support, can generate not only cultural value but meaningful economic impact.
"We often speak of art as culture, and rightly so. But art is also industry. It creates value, generates livelihoods, and shapes how a country is seen and understood beyond its borders," he said.
Also present was Ekuinas board member and chairman of the corporate social responsibility committee Datuk Abdul Mutalib Alis.
The programme provides emerging artists with mentorship, business training, market exposure and industry access, aimed at helping them build viable and sustainable careers in the creative economy.
"To succeed today, artists must also develop the ability to position their work, understand markets, build networks, and approach their practice with an entrepreneurial mindset," Omar said.
That view reflects a growing recognition that the creative economy is no longer peripheral to national development, but an increasingly important driver of jobs, innovation and global influence.
Iltizam Seni was launched to address a longstanding structural gap in Malaysia's art ecosystem; the access to networks, commercial literacy and institutional support needed by young artists to build sustainable careers.
This year, Ekuinas adopted a more focused model, working with a smaller cohort of resident artists to provide deeper mentorship, more structured development and stronger emphasis on discipline, resilience, reflection and business acumen alongside artistic exploration.
Their development also extended beyond the studio, with participants undergoing cultural immersion in Pattani, Thailand, aimed at broadening both perspective and practice.
The result is Alir, this year's culminating exhibition and the capstone of the five-month residency programme, featuring 22 works by resident artists Aliff Danial, Ammar Idris, Mairul Malek and Khairanee Iro.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid says BN lawmakers' move aimed at upholding royal institution, adat
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the move by all 14 Barisan Nasional assemblymen in Negri Sembilan to withdraw support for the Menteri Besar was made in good faith to ensure the state's royal institution and adat customs are respected and upheld by the people.
He said the action should not be misconstrued as an attempt to topple the state government or form a new administration through the back door.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreign property buyers turn to Malaysia amid geopolitical tensions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Ongoing geopolitical tensions are reshaping investor flows into Malaysia's property market with GD Properties seeing increased interest from foreign buyers, particularly from the Middle East.
GD Properties group chief executive Ng Khoon Hour said uncertainties in parts of the world have prompted investors to look toward more stable markets in Asia.
"It is encouraging to see renewed interest from Middle Eastern investors, many of whom are returning to Asia amid uncertainties in other regions," he said after launching KL360 @ Menara GD project here today.
Ng noted that some investors who had previously focused on markets such as Dubai are now diversifying geographically as conditions shift.
"The tensions around the world have built the expectation that Malaysia is a safe market," he said.
He added that demand from other regional markets including Thailand has also remained positive, reflecting broader confidence in Malaysia's economic and policy stability.
Citing recent international assessments, Ng said Malaysia is increasingly viewed as a safe haven within the region.
"From the foreign market side, we are cautiously optimistic. We are engaging both international and local agencies, and there is strong effort on the ground to market Malaysian properties," he added.
Despite global uncertainties, he said the company remains confident in the sector's resilience over the next 12 to 24 months, supported by sustained foreign interest and steady domestic demand.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: All 14 BN assemblymen to continue backing Negri Sembilan unity govt
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: All 14 Barisan Nasional assemblymen in Negri Sembilan have agreed to continue supporting the unity government in the state following a meeting with Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today.
Zahid said the decision was reached after discussions with the assemblymen, amid controversy surrounding their earlier withdrawal of support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
"My meeting with all the assemblymen showed that while we respect the decision made by the assemblymen concerned, the party at the national level and all the assemblymen I met today agreed that we should continue together with the unity government in Negri Sembilan so that there will be no disruption.
"This is for the benefit of the people as a whole," he said.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Probe into commando's critical injuries at camp completed, say police
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have completed their investigation into the case of a commando who suffered severe injuries at a military camp in Johor in March.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the investigation paper was submitted to the state prosecution director on Tuesday.
"We are now awaiting further instructions," he said when contacted, adding that a statement on the case would be issued today.
Ab Rahaman's comments follow demands by human rights group Lawyers for Liberty and the family of the commando, Abdul Hamid Talib, for a thorough investigation into the incident.
Hamid, who is attached to the 22nd Commando Regiment at Kem Iskandar in Mersing, reportedly sustained serious injuries on March 11 after a fall.
However, his family claims the injuries were sustained during a punishment at the camp.
Hamid is currently unable to speak and relies on a ventilator and feeding tube following serious head injuries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Huawei boosts Malaysia's smart energy push
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Huawei Digital Power has launched its FusionSolar 9.0 Smart photovoltaic (PV) and energy storage system (ESS) solution in Malaysia.
This marks a major shift from conventional solar systems to intelligent, grid-stabilising and artificial intelligence (AI)-driven energy infrastructure, boosting Malaysia's transition toward more intelligent, resilient and sustainable energy systems.
The solution introduces several industry firsts including the world's first grid-forming string inverter and the first 1000-volt alternating current PV system.
It also supports a maximum 11 megawatts (MW) PV array through a fundamental technological breakthrough.
In addition, AI will enable autonomous operations with minimal or no on-site personnel across mountainous, floating and remote island sites, while equipping each plant with self-optimisation capabilities.
The launch was hosted by Huawei Technologies (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd (Huawei Malaysia) and included 13 speakers from the green technology industry.
Huawei Malaysia executive vice president of digital power business Chong Chern Peng said Malaysia is making steady progress in its clean energy transition.
"Our ambition is clear, 70 per cent by 2050. Achieving this will require speed, scale and smarter technologies. This is exactly where Huawei FusionSolar 9.0 comes in," he said at the launch here today.
Chong added that by intelligently managing and storing solar energy, dependence on volatile fossil fuels can be reduced while lowering emissions, stabilising costs and building a stronger energy future for the nation.
Pushing the boundaries of utility-scale and large commercial and industrial (C&I) solar applications, the FusionSolar 9.0 solution features the latest industry-leading 506KTL Smart String Inverters and new generation 3000/7000/11,000kilovolt-ampere smart transformer station.
With AI-enabled scheduling, the system delivers up to 20 per cent higher energy yields and a round-trip efficiency of 91.3 per cent, maximising efficiency and returns.
Huawei claimed that the system's ability to stabilise power supply and switch between on-grid and off-grid modes helps reduce downtime and operational risks.
Its safety features, tested against international fire safety standards, ensures reliable performance in demanding environments across a wide array of industries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mamdani calls on King Charles to return Koh-i-Noor diamond
Article:
NEW YORK: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani called for King Charles to "return" the prized Koh-i-Noor diamond, which the British Empire took from the Indian subcontinent in the 1800s, on the third day of the monarch's state visit on Wednesday.
Before greeting Charles and Queen Camilla at a 9/11 memorial event, Mamdani was asked what he would discuss with the king if he had the chance.
"If I was to speak to the king, separately from that, I would probably encourage him to return the Koh-i-Noor diamond," the leftist mayor said, adding that his focus would be honoring those killed in the terror attacks.
It's unclear whether Mamdani followed through and brought up the contentious subject with Charles when the two met.
The monarch was seen laughing with Mamdani and having a brief conversation after they shook hands.
Housed in the Tower of London, the massive 106 carat stone is the star of Britain's crown jewels, adorning the Crown of Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother.
The ownership of the jewel has been contested over the centuries, passing through the hands of Mughal emperors, Iranian shahs and Sikh maharajas before the Kingdom of Punjab gave it to Queen Victoria in 1849 as part of a peace treaty.
India has repeatedly and unsuccessfully sought the return of the priceless jewel.
While there is little doubt it was mined in India, its history thereafter is a mixture of myth and fact, with several countries including Afghanistan, Iran and Pakistan also laying claim to the gem.
A politician from the anti-immigration Reform UK party was quick to slam the comments as an "insult to our King."
"This beautiful diamond is currently on display in the Tower of London," the party's home affairs spokesman Zia Yusuf said in an X post. "That is where it will stay."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thousands of bikers expected at RXZ Fighter 3.0 gathering in Kuantan
Article:
KUANTAN: Biking enthusiasts are set to converge on the state capital for the RXZ Fighter 3.0 gathering, which will be held at Serambi Teruntum in Tanjung Lumpur on May 1.
Serambi Teruntum hosted the inaugural edition in 2024,
which drew around 50,000 participants
, before the gathering was moved to Teluk Cempedak last year, where some 70,000 riders turned up.
Pahang Communications and Multimedia, Youth, Sports and Non-Governmental Organisations Committee chairman Fadzli Mohamad Kamal said Kuantan will once again play host to the two-day RXZ gathering, which begins tomorrow.
"I welcome RXZ fans from all over the country to Kuantan in conjunction with RXZ Fighter 3.0. Various interesting activities will be held throughout the event at Serambi Teruntum," he said in a video shared on Facebook today.
Fadzli advised the biking community travelling from across the country to Kuantan to prioritise safety at all times.
The event, scheduled to begin after Friday prayers, will include religious talks, bike-handling skills demonstrations by FIM MiniGP riders, an airbrush painting competition and performances by local artistes.
It marks the third time the event is being held in Kuantan,
despite calls to host it in other districts
, largely due to its potential positive spillover effects on tourism and the local economy.
Pahang Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Superintendent Brodie Ak Brangka has urged participants to observe road rules.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM230mil misappropriation case: NGO rep, company director on bail
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A deputy chairman of a non-governmental organisation and a company director have been released on bail following an investigation into the alleged misappropriation of RM230 million in NGO funds.
Sources said the duo, who allegedly conspired to transfer the funds into a company account for investment and personal use, were released after their remand period ended.
On April 25, the suspects had their remand
extended by five days
.
Selangor Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) director Datuk Mohd Azwan Ramli confirmed the release, adding that all suspects in the case are now out on MACC bail.
The case emerged earlier this month following MACC raids on several premises in Selangor and Kuala Lumpur, including offices linked to the NGO and the company.
Initial reports indicated that investigators are scrutinising financial transactions totalling RM230 million, believed to have been channelled into private accounts.
Several people, including NGO office bearers and company representatives, were detained in stages to facilitate inquiries.
Investigations are ongoing for criminal breach of trust and corruption.
While the NGO was not named, it was previously reported that  Pertubuhan Ikram Malaysia (Ikram) had
suspended
three officials amid the probe. It also pledged to cooperate with the MACC in the probe.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man heartbroken after failing to save disabled neighbour in fire
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 26-year-old man risked his life to rescue two elderly neighbours from a fire in Taman Ehsan early today, but was heartbroken after failing to save their disabled son.
The man, who only wanted to be known as Harvison, said he was asleep when he was awakened by shouts of fire from his neighbours.
"I was sleeping when the uncle from the fourth house shouted. He came to the front of my house and shouted about the fire.
"I thought my own house was on fire, but I ran out and saw the house behind me burning. I went back into my house, exited through the back door, and tried to enter their house through the kitchen.
"I jumped in, but the fire was already too big and had reached the back of the house. I managed to pull the elderly couple out," he said when met at the location today.
Harvison said he and several neighbours attempted to rescue the disabled man trapped in the bedroom, but their attempt failed as the fire was too intense.
"I am very tired and have not slept since 1am, but every time I close my eyes, I can still hear his voice screaming for help. It is truly sad.
"Although I am not very close to him — my mother said he has been paralysed since his 20s — I know the elderly couple very well. My siblings and I have known them since childhood as they bought the house at the same time as my father," he said.
The couple is currently staying at his family's home while their relatives find suitable accommodation.
Meanwhile, community activist Kuan Chee Heng, also known as Uncle Kentang, said his team would help the victims with temporary accommodation and necessities.
"For now, we will settle the accommodation issue because they are frail, have difficulty moving, and need someone to care for them.
"In the short term, we will place them at a hotel and manage other matters later," he said when met at the scene today.
Earlier, the Selangor Fire and Rescue Department reported that a 53-year-old man was found dead in the fire involving five terrace houses.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said they received a distress call at 2.31am before fire engines arrived at the scene at 2.50am.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jawi opposes Rain Rave Water Music Festival over moral concerns
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (Jawi) has opposed the proposed Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026, saying it could undermine moral values and social harmony.
In a statement today, Jawi expressed regret over the proposed programme and urged organisers to review its concept, stressing the need to respect Malaysia's multi-racial, multi-religious and cultural sensitivities.
It cited Bayan Linnas Series 351, a series of public religious rulings, guidance, and explanations, issued by the Federal Territories Mufti's Department, which states that entertainment is permissible in Islam but must comply with Syariah guidelines.
"Jawi regrets the proposed programme and urges organisers to reassess its implementation in line with societal sensitivities and Syariah requirements," it said.
The department said it will continue to implement religious programmes in the Federal Territories through lectures, forums, talks, social media campaigns and Friday sermons to promote Islamic teachings and discourage activities deemed immoral.
It added that Yassin recitations and solat hajat will be held simultaneously across Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and Labuan after Maghrib today, seeking blessings and harmony for the people and the country.
Jawi also urged all parties to ensure entertainment programmes are free from elements that could affect religious sanctity and public morals.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] IGP urges calm amid Negri Sembilan Adat Perpatih crisis
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail has urged all quarters to remain calm and refrain from making unnecessary statements over the crisis involving Negri Sembilan's Adat Perpatih customary system and its constitutional framework.
He said issuing false or inaccurate statements could undermine efforts to restore the situation to normal.
He said numerous reports related to issues involving religion, race and the royal institution (3R) had been received in connection with the crisis in Negri Sembilan.
"I can confirm that many reports have been received from the relevant parties, including those expressing dissatisfaction and related matters.
"We will conduct investigations before deciding on the next course of action," he said.
On April 27, 14 Negri Sembilan Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen
withdrew their support
for the state government led by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, citing a crisis involving the state's Adat Perpatih customary system and its constitutional framework.
Negri Sembilan Umno Liaison Committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias had said the party leadership had taken note of developments involving the state's Adat Perpatih system and its interaction with the state constitutional framework.
Jalaluddin, who is also the Jelebu member of parliament and Pertang assemblyman, said Umno/BN executive council members were not informed of any discussions at the state level and only became aware of the crisis when it had escalated into public debate.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Police nab 69 in nationwide crackdown on online child sexual exploitation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have detained 69 people, including civil servants, in a nationwide crackdown on online child sexual exploitation under Operation Cyber Guardian 2026.
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the operation was part of a multi-national effort involving seven countries: Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Brunei, Hong Kong, South Korea and Japan.
"Those detained, aged between 15 and 66, were from various backgrounds and occupations, following coordinated nationwide raids from April 6 to 8 targeting 101 individuals identified through intelligence.
"Of the total, 67 were original targets while two others were accomplices found at the locations during inspections," he said at a press conference at the federal police headquarters in Bukit Aman here.
Khalid said the operation was carried out based on integrated intelligence conducted by the Criminal Investigation Department's Sexual, Women and Child Investigation Division (D11) Malaysia Internet Crime Against Children (MICAC), as well as cross-border intelligence sharing with enforcement agencies from the countries involved.
Khalid said inspections were conducted on all targets, including their premises and digital devices, which led to the seizures of 126 items, including mobile phones, laptops and storage devices, as well as two sex toys.
"Preliminary checks also uncovered 498,694 digital files, comprising 204,934 child sexual abuse material (CSAM) files and 293,760 adult pornographic files," he said.
Khalid said investigations revealed that the suspects used structured digital networks to access, store and distribute such materials across various online platforms, including encrypted communication applications.
"Some suspects acted as group administrators, offering access to CSAM for payment via e-wallet transactions and QR codes, indicating a commercial element in these crimes.
"These crimes are no longer isolated acts but have evolved into organised, profit-driven activities," he said.
Khalid said they had opened 67 investigation papers under various laws, including the Sexual Offences Against Children Act, the Penal Code and the Communications and Multimedia Act.
"Of these, 62 cases involving 62 accused persons have been charged with a total of 81 charges.
"Among those charged, two individuals had been sentenced to imprisonment of between three and seven months, while others were fined or are awaiting court proceedings," he said.
Khalid added that the operation involved a total of 339 police personnel, comprising 72 senior officers and 243 rank-and-file personnel.
It was also supported by 24 officers from the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission, who provided technical assistance in communication and digital network aspects.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China challenges Western model, pitches its path to modernisation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: China is ready to share its experience of modernisation with developing countries and support them in charting their own paths based on national conditions, China's Ambassador to Malaysia Ouyang Yujing said.
He said China's development had shown that modernisation does not necessarily equate to "Westernisation", stressing that there was no single model applicable to all countries.
"There is no standard model for modernisation, nor is there a universally applicable formula," he said at the China-Malaysia Readers Forum on the English edition of Xi Jinping: The Governance of China (Volume V).
The event was hosted by the State Council Information Office of China, the China International Communications Group and the Chinese Embassy in Malaysia.
Among those present were Transport Minister Anthony Loke, Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul and China International Communications Group president Chang Bo.
Ouyang said China's development path could serve as a reference for other developing nations.
"President Xi Jinping has pointed out that modernisation is the right and inevitable choice for all countries. The key is to find a path suited to national conditions and the laws of social development," he said.
He added that China was prepared to deepen strategic cooperation with Malaysia as both countries enter new phases of their national development plans.
"This year marks the first year of China's 15th Five-Year Plan and Malaysia's 13th Malaysia Plan.
"China is willing to enhance strategic alignment and policy communication with Malaysia, strengthen exchanges and mutual learning in governance, and jointly advance to a new stage of modernisation," he said.
Ouyang noted that both countries share aligned development philosophies and are highly complementary in terms of industrial structures and strengths.
"Malaysia is China's second-largest trading partner in Asean and its largest source of imports, while China has remained Malaysia's largest trading partner for 17 consecutive years.
"China will work with Malaysia to implement the outcomes of President Xi's visit last year and expand cooperation in emerging sectors such as the digital and green economy, as well as advanced manufacturing and artificial intelligence.
"Together, we will build a regional hub in new quality productive forces, support each other's industrial transformation and upgrading, and accelerate the modernisation process," he said.
He added that recent high-level visits, including Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's trip to China in 2023 and President Xi's visit to Malaysia, had elevated bilateral ties.
"Malaysia has actively joined the Group of Friends of the Global Governance Initiative initiated by China.
"The flagship Belt and Road Initiative project, the East Coast Rail Link, has entered its final construction stage. It is the largest single overseas infrastructure project undertaken by a Chinese company.
"Under the strategic guidance of both countries' leaders, another 'golden 50 years' of China-Malaysia relations is within reach," he said.
Meanwhile, Loke said the book offered insight into China's approach to governance and development.
"In many ways, it is an act of friendship… an invitation to understand how another nation thinks about its future.
"In it, you will see how China plans for poverty eradication, food security, energy security and modernisation.
"These are concerns shared by many countries today, but in China, they are planned well in advance," he said.
He added that such governance approaches were worth reflecting on, particularly in managing a population of 1.4 billion people while ensuring stability and prosperity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teenager fined RM3,000 for injuring traffic policeman at roadblock
Article:
KLANG: A teenager was fined RM3,000 by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to injuring a traffic policeman at a roadblock on Persiaran Raja Muda Musa on Monday.
Magistrate Siti Zubaidah Mahat handed down the sentence to Muhammad Irfan Jasman, 19, after he pleaded guilty to the charge.
The court also ordered the accused to serve three months in jail if he fails to pay the fine.
According to the charge, Muhammad Irfan intentionally caused injury to Corporal Ahmad Faidz Mat Jusoh while the policeman was carrying out his duties at the location at 1am on April 27.
The charge under Section 332 of the Penal Code carries a maximum imprisonment of three years, a fine, or both, upon conviction.
In mitigation, lawyer K. Nathiya said her client is a young offender who earns an unstable income as a part-time delivery rider and supports his parents.
"This is the accused's first offence. I urge the court to impose a minimum fine," she said.
Deputy public prosecutor Salman Alfarisi Nawawi requested an appropriate sentence, taking into account that the incident occurred in a public place and resulted in rib injuries to the victim who was merely performing his duties.
In a separate court, Muhammad Irfan also pleaded guilty to riding recklessly at 1.30am on the same date and at the same location.
The charge was submitted by deputy public prosecutor Nur Nadia Syuhada Rosli under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Upon conviction, the offence carries a penalty of up to five years imprisonment and a fine of up to RM15,000, in addition to being disqualified from holding or obtaining a driving licence.
Magistrate A. Khartiyayini granted bail of RM4,000 with one surety and set June 11 for the social report.
Yesterday, Klang Selatan police chief Assistant Commissioner Lim Jit Huey said the incident occurred when a teenage couple attempted to flee the roadblock by ramming the officer.
The impact of the crash caused the 39-year-old police officer to be thrown and suffer head and facial injuries, while the rider and pillion passenger suffered minor injuries.
"Both suspects were detained, and they tried to flee because they did not have a driving licence," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreigner pleads guilty to possessing obscene material on phone
Article:
PONTIAN: A foreigner pleaded guilty at the Pontian magistrate's court today to charges of possessing obscene material and nude videos on his mobile phone on April 26.
The accused, Santosh Mukhiya, 24, a Nepali, said he understood the charges read out before magistrate Nurhafiza Haron. He was unrepresented.
Earlier, Mukhiya, who is single and employed by a company in the district, arrived at the court at 8.40am accompanied by investigating officer Inspector Fakhrul Naim Md Lias from the Commercial Crime Investigation Division of the Pontian district police headquarters.
According to the charge sheet, at 11am on April 26, the complainant, whose address is in Kampung Parit Hj Arif, Kayu Ara Pasong, Pontian, was informed by a colleague who had seen a screenshot posted on the accused's social media status.
The accused is also the complainant's former boyfriend.
The video recording allegedly showed the complainant naked while engaging in explicit acts with the accused.
Acting on the information, police arrested the accused, who works for a private company in Pekan Nanas, and seized an orange Honor X9B mobile phone.
Further inspection by the police uncovered obscene video recordings stored in a folder on the accused's phone.
The case was investigated under Section 292 of the Penal Code for possession of obscene material, which carries a jail term of up to three years, a fine, or both.
The court later imposed a fine of RM5,000, in default five months' jail.
Mukhiya paid the fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Immigration extends migrant repatriation programme to May 2027
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has extended the Migrant Repatriation Programme 2.0 (PRM 2.0) from May 1 to May 31, 2027, after it was due to end today.
In a statement, it said the decision followed a positive assessment of the programme, citing strong registrations, steady voluntary returns and encouraging compound collections.
Between May 19, 2025 and April 29, 2026, the
programme recorded 254,186 registrations
involving migrants from 112 countries, with total collections amounting to RM127 million.
PRM 2.0 is a voluntary amnesty initiative that allows undocumented migrants to return to their home countries without facing prosecution.
Compounds are set at RM500 for illegal entry or overstaying, RM300 for pass violations, along with a RM20 special pass fee.
"The extension aims to boost voluntary compliance, expedite structured repatriation and optimise enforcement resources," it said.
It added that enforcement will be intensified nationwide through integrated operations, intelligence gathering and monitoring to detect, detain, prosecute and deport offenders, including employers and harbourers.
The department urged undocumented migrants to come forward and regularise their status through legal channels before enforcement action is taken.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia Airlines to resume Doha flights on July 2
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines Bhd will resume limited services to Doha  from July 2, following Qatar's reopening of its airspace to international carriers and the gradual reinstatement of operations at Hamad International Airport (HIA).
Malaysia Airlines said the reopening of operations follows a "notice to airmen" (Notam) by Qatar Civil Aviation Authority confirming operational readiness.
The international airline services are now gradually resuming, with initial flights operating through a designated flight corridor coordinated by the Qatari authority.
"Malaysia Airlines operations will initially be limited to one daily service (MH160/161).
"The remaining frequencies (MH164/165 and MH168/169) will continue to be suspended until Oct 24 2026, subject to ongoing review," it said.
The Doha service was initially suspended from Feb 28 due to airspace closures affecting the region.
"The airline continues to closely monitor the situation through ongoing risk assessments and relevant Notam before fully resuming operations," Malaysia Airlines added.
Amal by Malaysia Airlines, the haj and umrah pilgrimage arm of Malaysia Aviation Group, operates flights to Jeddah and Madinah as scheduled to accommodate the 2026 haj season with a total of 110 flights across two phases, transporting a combined total of 15,620 pilgrims.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tourism ministry to comply with spending cuts, keep Visit Malaysia 2026 plan
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry (Motac) said it will comply with Treasury expenditure cuts while maintaining key initiatives, including Visit Malaysia 2026, as planned.
The ministry's secretary-general Datuk Shaharuddin Abu Sohot said Motac would fully comply with the government's directive on expenditure controls, as outlined by the Finance Ministry, in line with efforts to ensure prudent fiscal management.
"Motac emphasises that the approach aligns with the government's priority of maintaining responsible fiscal governance, with national interest remaining the core consideration in all spending decisions," he said.
Shaharuddin's assurance comes amid concerns over how cost-cutting measures across ministries could affect major national campaigns, particularly Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026), which has been extended to the end of 2027 to further drive tourism recovery and growth.
The Treasury has called on ministries to reassess operating expenditure as part of efforts to strengthen Malaysia's fiscal position amid global economic uncertainty.
Despite the tighter spending environment, he said Motac remains committed to implementing strategic initiatives within the tourism sector, describing VM2026 as a key national agenda to stimulate economic expansion and strengthen Malaysia's position as a leading global destination.
To balance fiscal constraints with policy priorities, he said the ministry will adopt more targeted and disciplined spending controls, including optimising existing resources and prioritising high-impact programmes.
"This approach ensures that every ringgit spent delivers maximum returns to the economy and benefits to the rakyat," Shaharuddin added.
He also said the implementation of VM2026 will continue to be guided by the principles of "national interest" and "public interest", with a focus on delivering tangible gains for the public, industry players and the broader economy.
"At Motac, we are confident that, through a disciplined and strategic approach, the targets and aspirations of VM2026 can still be achieved despite the current fiscal tightening measures," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sisters' drug use uncovered in Klang raid
Article:
KLANG: A woman who introduced drugs to her sister in a bid to help her lose weight has led both of them deeper into substance abuse.
The woman, in her 40s, was detained by the Selangor National Anti-Drugs Agency (Nada) at an abandoned building settlement in Bandar Armada Putra here today.
She admitted to introducing methamphetamine to her sister, also in her 40s, several years ago, and said she has tried, but failed,to quit the habit.
"I have been taking drugs for a long time, since my 20s. My sister initially only took medication because she wanted to lose weight, but I gave her this to try.
"Eventually, we became addicted and sometimes took the drugs together," she said after being detained during Op Perdana with her boyfriend. Her sister was also detained in the same unit alongside her husband.
The sister said Nada had previously detained them.
She added that she did not consume the substance daily, spending about RM20 to obtain the drugs, or sometimes receiving them for free when using them with friends.
"Initially, I did not know my sister was taking drugs. I used to be overweight and wanted to slim down, which is why I tried it," she said.
She is a grandmother of two.
Meanwhile, a single mother detained alongside several men in another unit in the same building expressed regret over her involvement in drugs, saying her daughter was unaware of her activities.
She said she had been abusing drugs for the past two to three months, adding that the methamphetamine was supplied by her boyfriend for RM50.
"I work part-time with a delivery company and live here with my boyfriend. My child does not know I take drugs; I hide in the room to take them while she plays outside," she said.
Earlier, the Op Perdana raid, led by the Selangor Nada and launched at 4.30am, was carried out in collaboration with the Selangor Immigration Department and the National Registration Department.
A total of 61 individuals tested positive for drugs and were detained.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Father jailed for raping teenage daughter while she slept
Article:
MUAR: A 37-year-old man was sentenced to 20 years' imprisonment and 13 strokes of the cane by the Sessions Court here today after pleading guilty to raping and sexually assaulting his 15-year-old daughter.
Judge Mohd Khairi Haron handed down the sentence after considering the facts of the case, the accused's admission of guilt and submissions by the prosecution.
According to the charges, the accused, a grass cutter, committed sexual assault on the victim while she was asleep in her bedroom at their home in Tangkak between 8pm and 9pm last year.
He was charged under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, read together with Section 16 of the same Act, which provides for imprisonment of up to 20 years and whipping.
In a separate charge, he was accused of raping the victim at the same house between 2am and 3am on Feb 23.
The offence, under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code, carries a sentence of up to 30 years' imprisonment and a minimum of 10 strokes of the cane.
Facts of the case revealed that the accused had entered the victim's room while she was asleep and covered her mouth during the assault.
The abuse came to light on April 20 when the victim's mother received a call from the school informing her of the incident. The victim was then taken to hospital for examination.
Police investigations established that the victim had been assaulted on two occasions — last year and again in February — both while she was asleep in her room.
Acting on information, police from the Tangkak district police headquarters arrested the accused at about 1pm on April 20 and seized his mobile phone to assist in investigations.
Deputy public prosecutor Nur Ameerah Allaudeen appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.
In addition to the prison sentence, the court ordered the accused to undergo counselling during his incarceration and be placed under police supervision for two years upon release, in accordance with Sections 26 and 27 of the Act.
In a separate case reported yesterday,
a 47-year-old labourer in Batu Pahat pleaded guilty to raping his 20-year-old daughter, who was later found to be 18 weeks pregnant.
He faces up to 30 years' imprisonment and a minimum of 10 strokes of the cane.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Letter ordering vacation of Undang Luak Sungei Ujong's residence circulates
Article:
SEREMBAN: A letter purportedly from the Negri Sembilan State Secretary's Office is circulating, directing the immediate vacation of the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong official residence and the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.
The April 29 letter is addressed to the private secretary of Datuk Mubarak Dohak, who was stripped of his position as the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong earlier this month.
The letter cites a decision made during a special session of the Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) on April 17.
"Accordingly, you are directed to vacate the official residence and Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong with immediate effect. All state government assets registered at the premises must be maintained in good condition for future inspection," the letter read.
State Secretary Datuk Mohd Zafir Ibrahim declined to comment when contacted, though he did not deny the letter's existence.
"I cannot comment on anything," he said.
A check at the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong today found the premises quiet with no staff present.
It is understood that state government employees stationed there have been reassigned.
On April 17, the DKU accepted the removal of Mubarak as the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong following a special meeting at Istana Besar Seri Menanti in Kuala Pilah.
The session was presided over by the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
Yesterday, it was
reported
that Mubarak and the three other Undang held a meeting at the Balai Luak Sungei Ujong amid an ongoing dispute with Tuanku Muhriz.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia Q1 net profit up 6.4pct to RM72.8mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's net profit rose 6.4 per cent to RM72.83 million in the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026 from RM68.42 million a year ago.
The company's revenue rose to RM214.08 million in Q1 2026 from RM184.38 million in the comparative quarter.
This was supported by robust core-market activity and sustained momentum in initiatives to strengthen participation and market vibrancy.
In a statement, Bursa Malaysia said its operating revenue grew 16.4 per cent year on year (YoY) to RM206.9 million.
This was supported mainly by higher securities trading activity as well as increased listing and issuer fees.
Bursa Malaysia's operating expenses increased 24.2 per cent to RM115.3 million, largely due to higher regulatory-related fees and continued expenditure in talent and technology.
In the Islamic market, the exchange said Bursa Suq Al-Sila' (BSAS) recorded an average daily value of RM50.7billion, supported by broad participation and liquidity.
In the securities market, average daily trading value for on-market trades rose 27.0 per cent YoY to RM3.3 billion, supported by broad participation across investor segments.
"Fundraising activity was encouraging in Q1 2026, with 16 initial public offering (IPOs) across the main, ACE and Leap markets.
"The total market capitalisation increased by 11.4 per cent YoY to RM2.1 trillion in Q1 2026," it said.
Bursa Malaysia chief executive officer Datuk Fad'l Mohamed said the higher trading activity and strong listing pipeline reflect confidence in the local capital market.
Building on the performance, Fad'l said the market development priorities were progressed to strengthen the quality, accessibility and breadth of the capital market ecosystem.
On the outlook, Fad'l said Malaysia's capital market continues to operate within a resilient domestic environment, supported by steady economic growth and policy certainty.
With gross development product (GDP) growth projected at 4.0 per cent to 5.0 per cent, he said the backdrop provides a constructive foundation for investment and capital market activity.
"Against this landscape, Bursa Malaysia remains focused on fulfilling its role as a trusted market operator, strengthening market vibrancy and supporting sustainable capital formation to meet the evolving needs of investors,issuers and the broader economy," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khaled: Defence cuts will not affect readiness
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said planned operating expenditure cuts will not significantly affect the country's defence readiness and capability.
He said the ministry would adjust its priorities under the revised allocation.
"We will make sure the cut does not significantly affect our readiness and capabilities.
"We will rearrange our priorities. In general, we fully understand why the measures are taken and believe that all have to share and make sacrifices.
"Every ministry needs to chip in," he told the New Straits Times.
Yesterday, the Finance Ministry proposed a RM5.4 billion reduction in operating expenditure across government sectors as part of efforts to strengthen the country's fiscal position.
The move is part of a broader plan to achieve RM10 billion in operating expenditure savings this year across ministries, departments and government agencies, in line with preparations for the 2026 Budget.
Treasury guidelines seen by Berita Harian indicated the measures were in response to developments linked to the Middle East crisis.
Geopolitical instability and ongoing conflicts in the region, along with disruptions to key shipping routes such as the Strait of Hormuz, which carries about 20 per cent of global oil flows, have led to significant supply disruptions and a sharp rise in global crude oil prices.
The Defence Ministry is expected to see a RM508 million reduction in operating expenditure.
Ministries and agencies have been instructed to reprioritise spending and submit their savings proposals to the National Budget Office by May 15.
For 2026, the Defence Ministry and the armed forces have been allocated RM21.74 billion in total, marking an increase of about 2.92 per cent compared with RM21.13 billion in the previous year.
Of the total, RM14.11 billion is allocated for operating expenditure, while RM7.63 billion is for development expenditure.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Army major claims trial to RM225,000 false claims charges
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: An army major claimed trial at the Ayer Kerod Sessions Court here today to five charges of submitting false claims involving maintenance work and non-existent raw material supplies worth over RM225,000 two years ago.
Major D. Vivekananthan, 43, entered the plea after the charges under Section 18 of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission Act 2009 were read out before Judge Elesabet Paya Wan.
According to the charge sheet, the accused, while serving as the Commanding Officer of the 9th Squadron of the Royal Engineers Regiment (9 SKN RAJD) at Terendak Camp, allegedly used five invoices from different companies containing false details related to construction and maintenance work.
For the first charge, he is accused of using an invoice under the name Jelatok Rimba Enterprise, dated March 12, 2024, involving the construction of an Armed Forces Trade Training Workshop, two troop squad huts, and a C-type vehicle training store amounting to RM80,000, which did not actually exist.
The second charge involves the use of an invoice from WH Technic Resources, dated March 26, 2024, for the maintenance of the Guardhouse Garage (BPKP 21) and related work for the 9 SKN RAJD team, valued at RM35,523.75, which was also allegedly not carried out.
For the remaining three charges, the accused is alleged to have used false invoices from Sri Bistari Trading (RM20,000), Subsurf Sdn Bhd (RM39,950), and Fareena Universal (RM49,545) for the maintenance of the 9 SKN RAJD FAMTO store, a historical room, and troop accommodation blocks, none of which were carried out.
All the offences were allegedly committed at the 9 SKN RAJD Office at Terendak Camp between March 12 and Nov 19, 2024.
If convicted, he faces a maximum of 20 years in prison and a fine of at least five times the value of the false information or RM10,000, whichever is higher, under Section 24(2) of the same act.
Deputy public prosecutor Sharina Farhana Nor Sa'ari requested that the accused be denied bail, citing the seriousness of the offences.
Defence counsel P. Thisharmini. appealed for low bail, arguing that her client is the sole breadwinner for his family, including his wife, a six-year-old child, and his parents-in-law.
The court then allowed the accused bail of RM20,000 with one surety, alongside the additional conditions requested by the prosecution.
The case was set for mention again on June 5 for document submission.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Commando critically injured after alleged punishment, LFL urges attempted murder classification
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Human rights group Lawyers for Liberty (LFL) has demanded an immediate explanation from the government over the severe injuries sustained by a commando, who is now in critical condition after allegedly being subjected to punishment at a military camp in Johor in March.
LFL lawyer N Surendran said the family and legal team have yet to receive any satisfactory explanation regarding the incident involving Truper Abdul Hamid Talib, 25.
He said the victim is currently in critical condition, unconscious, unable to speak, and reliant on medical assistance for breathing and nutrition via a feeding tube.
"The victim also suffered severe head injuries, with part of his skull having to be removed. This places him at risk of permanent disability," he told a press conference at the LFL office here today.
Also present were the victim's mother, Josmah Duim, 54; sister, Noreliana Talib, 30; and brother, Saifudin Sajali, 29.
Surendran said information received indicates that the injuries occurred while punishment was being carried out at the camp on March 11.
For the record, Abdul Hamid serves with the 22nd Commando Regiment at Kem Iskandar, Mersing, Johor, and hails from Bum Bum, Semporna, Sabah.
"We were informed that the injuries occurred during punishment, not as a result of a fall as previously stated.
"To date, no official explanation has been given by the military or the government on how the victim ended up in such a condition," he said.
He said a police report on the incident was lodged by the family on March 14 at the Mersing police station, but no updates on the investigation have been provided.
"No charges have been filed and no official statement has been issued, despite the seriousness of the case.
"We see a failure to provide answers to the family and the public," he said.
Surendran said the group has put forward several demands to the government and authorities.
"The government, the Defence Ministry and military leadership must provide a full explanation of what happened to the victim.
"We also call on the Inspector-General of Police to deploy a special team from Bukit Aman to immediately investigate the case," he said.
He added that all personnel suspected of involvement should be suspended pending investigations to ensure transparency.
"Suspension is crucial to ensure the investigation can proceed without interference. The case should also be classified as attempted murder, given the severity of the victim's injuries," he said.
Surendran further urged that all responsible parties be prosecuted in court regardless of rank.
"We want to see prosecution so that incidents like this do not recur. We also urge the government to establish a royal commission of inquiry to investigate the matter comprehensively," he said.
He said a full investigation is necessary to determine whether unlawful physical punishment practices exist within military camps.
"If such physical punishment was indeed carried out, it would be contrary to armed forces regulations. We want the truth revealed and justice for the victim's family," he said.
Meanwhile, lawyer representing the family, Suzana Norlihan Alias, claimed that the camp had attempted to restrict the family's access to the victim's medical reports.
She said the reports were only obtained after pressure was exerted on the camp authorities.
"The camp gave the excuse that it could not release the report for fear that the case would go to court. This raises questions as to whether there were attempts to prevent the family from obtaining the truth," she said.
Suzana said she also received information from several individuals alleging that unlawful physical punishment was practised within the unit.
"The information received suggests that physical punishment is not uncommon and has become routine.
"This must be investigated as it contradicts existing regulations," she said.
She added that the family has also not
received adequate assistance or compensation to cope with the victim's critical condition.
"Abdul Hamid now requires intensive care, and the family has had to bear the costs themselves, including special milk and disposable diapers.
"This has added to the emotional and financial burden of the family," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Elderly man suffers severe burns in Kuantan workshop fire
Article:
KUANTAN: An elderly man suffered severe burns after a lorry workshop was partially destroyed in a fire at Lorong Bukit Setongkol here today.
The 68-year-old victim, believed to be a worker at the premises, sustained burns to about 60 per cent of his body and is receiving treatment in the red zone at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital.
Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said the Kuantan fire station was alerted to the incident at 8.45am.
"Thirty-five firefighters from the Kuantan, Taman Tas and Indera Mahkota stations were deployed. About 70 per cent of the workshop was destroyed.
"Investigations found the victim was working alone at the time and had attempted to extinguish the fire himself. He sustained burns across his body," he said in a statement.
Salahuddin said the fire was brought under control at about 9.10am, and investigations into the cause are ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Transport Ministry extends Madani30 30pc ETS, ERT discount to May 14 after strong response
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The 30 per cent discount offered under the Madani30 promotional code for Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) Electric Train Service (ETS) and Ekspres Rakyat Timuran (ERT) services has been extended until May 14.
The Ministry of Transport (MOT), in a statement today, said the extension was made following encouraging public response, reflecting the public’s readiness to shift towards more affordable and convenient rail transport.
“For the travel period from May to October 2026, a total of 1.2 million seats are being offered under the Madani30 initiative, providing ample opportunity for the public to plan their journeys early at more reasonable fares.
“In this regard, MOT urges the public to take advantage of this opportunity by planning their trips in advance and using KTMB services to their preferred destinations,” it said.
According to MOT, the initiative remains available for the purchase of adult tickets across all ETS sectors as well as ERT services on the JB Sentral-Tumpat-JB Sentral route, for travel up to Oct 14 this year.
The discount is valid for travel from Mondays to Thursdays, except for school holidays and public holidays.
MOT said the initiative is part of the government’s ongoing efforts to strengthen the ‘road to rail’ agenda, which is the transition from road to rail, which is more sustainable, efficient and environmentally friendly.
“The use of rail transport is not only more comfortable and safe, but also contributes to environmental sustainability and reduces traffic congestion.
“This approach is in line with the Madani Government’s aspirations in reducing road congestion, reducing carbon footprint, and improving the quality of life of the people through a more integrated public transport system,” the ministry said.
The public can get more information via KTMB’s official social media page and make advance purchases via the KTMB Mobile application, KITS Style, KTMB’s official website or ticket kiosks at selected stations.
MOT also stressed the government’s commitment to continue strengthening the national rail transport ecosystem as the backbone of Malaysia’s future mobility.  — Bernama

Title: PDRM seizes nearly 500,000 illicit files in sweeping child exploitation crackdown across Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) announced today the seizure of 498,694 digital files in a single sweeping crackdown on online child sexual exploitation in Malaysia, carried out as part of a recent seven-country operation targeting cross-border networks involved in the sharing of child sexual abuse material (CSAM).
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said 69 out of 101 identified CSAM enablers were tracked down and arrested in Malaysia following extensive intelligence-sharing with regional and international law enforcement partners.
The transborder operation, dubbed Operasi Cyber Guardian 2026, saw participation from six other countries, namely Brunei, Japan, South Korea, Hong Kong, Thailand and Singapore.
With the main operation taking place from March 23 to April 17, Mohd Khalid said PDRM alongside its state contingents conducted simultaneous arrests nationwide between April 6 and April 8.
“Searches were performed on the premises and electronic devices of those targeted and our profiling of the identified perpetrators found that they came from various backgrounds.
Those arrested were aged between 15 and 66, with all except one being male.
“A total of 124 electronic devices alongside 204,934 CSAM and 293,760 adult pornography files were also seized,” he told a press conference at the federal police headquarters in Bukit Aman here.
A total of 339 personnel from the PDRM, including the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) which provided technical support, were mobilised.
According to Mohd Khalid, 171 online platforms and groups were found to be involved in the distribution and sale of CSAM in Malaysia following the crackdown.
“Some of those arrested were group administrators who offered paid access to CSAM via e-Wallet and QR payment which showed element of commercialisation by utilising the digital ecosystem to access, store and distribute CSAM,” he said.
The breakdown of arrests according to states was Selangor (22), Kedah and Johor (10); Penang, Perak and Pahang (9); Perlis and Negeri Sembilan (8); KL (5); Kelantan, Terengganu and Sarawak (3); Sabah and Melaka (1).
Following the arrests, Mohd Khalid said 67 investigation papers (IPs) were opened, with 62 leading to prosecutions, two still under investigation, and three classified as requiring no further action.
Mohd Khalid said the latest crackdown demonstrates PDRM’s consistent, uncompromised and stern enforcement against online child sexual exploitation crimes.
“Such crimes are no longer individual in nature, but have evolved into more organised, profit-driven networks operating across borders.
“PDRM views these developments seriously as online child sexual exploitation is becoming more complex, structured and wide-ranging, requiring a comprehensive approach involving intelligence, enforcement and strategic cooperation at both national and international levels,” he said.
Mohd Khalid also reiterated that strict and uncompromising action will be taken against any party involved, while urging the public to strengthen digital awareness and closely monitor children’s use of digital devices to reduce exposure to online threats.
“We are committed to strengthening strategic collaboration with local and overseas agencies to ensure children are protected from any form of exploitation and abuse,” he said.

Title: Criminals, traffic violators beware: DBKL’s 10,000 CCTVs can now identify faces, track your routes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The Kuala Lumpur City Hall today said all of its 10,000 CCTV cameras have been upgraded with high resolution facial recognition technology that could help authorities monitor, identify law breakers and respond swiftly.
The upgrade, announced by Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and DBKL Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud here, is part of a large-scale roll out of an integrated system that would connect monitoring, enforcement and city management capabilities.
“This is part of efforts to make the city safer,” Yeoh said at a press conference to unveil the system.
“If you think about doing crime in KL, don’t.”
Also present was Kuala Lumpur Police Chief, CP Datuk Fadil Marsus.
MORE TO COME

Title: Search for 10-year-old boy who fell in Puncak Alam drain ends after three days; body found in Sungai Buloh
Article:
SHAH ALAM, April 30 — The body of a 10-year-old boy, who went missing after reportedly falling into a drain in Bandar Puncak Alam on Tuesday (28), was found in Sungai Buloh today.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mokhtar said the body was found at about 1 pm, about six kilometres (km) from where he was reported to have fallen at Lorong Cakera Purnama 12/27.
He said the boy’s body was recovered by rescue personnel and handed over to the police.
“For the third day of operation, which began early this morning, the rescue personnel conducted surface water searches, as well as diving operations at two key areas leading towards the river.
“This integrated operation also involved the JBPM’s Water Rescue Unit (PPDA) and K9 Tracker Dog Unit,” he said in a statement today.
Earlier, the victim was reported to have been swept away by strong currents during heavy rain in an incident that occurred at about 3.30 pm on Tuesday, before firefighters received an emergency call at 7.47 pm. — Bernama

Title: What happened at Johor military camp? Family questions how healthy soldier ended up in vegetative state
Article:
PETALING JAYA, April 30 —  The family of a Grup Gerak Khas (GGK) personnel from the 22 Commando Regiment, Trooper Abdul Hamid Talib, has urged the government to provide an explanation and conduct a thorough investigation into the severe injuries he sustained while at camp.
Lawyers for Liberty (LFL) adviser N. Surendran, who is also the family’s spokesperson, said at a press conference today that the family had yet to receive a reasonable explanation as to how the 25-year-old victim suffered such serious injuries, leaving him in a vegetative state.
He said the victim, who was previously healthy, was reported to have sustained severe injuries on March 11 while at a military camp in Mersing, Johor.
“The victim is currently receiving treatment at the University of Malaya Medical Centre after being transferred from several other hospitals,” he said during the press conference held at the LFL office here today.
Also present were the family’s legal representative Suzana Norlihan Alias, the victim’s mother Josmah Duim, 54, his brother Saifudin Talib, 30, and his sister Noreliana, 29.
Surendran said a police report was lodged by Saifudin on March 14, but there had been no significant progress in the case so far.
As such, he said the family wanted the Ministry of Defence and the leadership of the Malaysian Armed Forces to provide a comprehensive explanation regarding the actual incident, and also called on the police to carry out an immediate investigation.
“The family is also demanding that all officers and personnel involved be suspended pending investigations to ensure transparency,” he said.
Meanwhile, Josmah demanded justice over what had happened to her son, describing the incident as unacceptable.
“I sent my son in a healthy condition, and suddenly he ended up like this. I cannot accept it,” she said.
Petaling Jaya District Police chief Shamsudin Mamat, when contacted, confirmed that family members had lodged a second police report at the Petaling Jaya district police headquarters today. — Bernama

Title: Witness tells court former Perlis MB Azlan Man received RM1.06m from cancelled state-funded travel packages
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The Sessions Court was told today that there were elements of misappropriation of Perlis state government property when Datuk Seri Azlan Man received funds resulting from the cancellation of overseas travel packages for trips that never took place.
Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Senior Superintendent M. Theenmoly, 41, testified that investigations revealed Azlan — through his then-Senior Private Secretary, Herwardy Hashim — had instructed the agencies Sri Kedawang Travel & Tour (W) Sdn Bhd (SKTT) and Aidil Travel & Tours Sdn Bhd to cancel the travel bookings.
“Datuk Seri Azlan Man then made claims for the money paid by the Perlis State Government, which was subsequently deposited into his bank account,” she said during the trial of the former Perlis Menteri Besar, who faces 10 charges of submitting false claims and receiving proceeds from illegal activities.
The 20th prosecution witness stated that, acting on Azlan’s instructions, the two travel agencies returned the funds from the cancelled vacations — totalling RM1.06 million — into Azlan’s bank account via four cheques and cash between February 14, 2014, and January 24, 2017.
“My investigation is based on documentary evidence such as local orders, bank statements from both parties, and witness testimony. Therefore, I found evidence showing that Datuk Seri Azlan Man received money from illegal activities, namely the cancellation of overseas vacation packages,” she said.
Theenmoly added that according to testimony from the then-Secretary of the Perlis State Executive Council, Norazlan Yahya, no consideration papers were presented to obtain approval to convert the overseas leave facilities approved for Azlan into cash for the years 2013 to 2017.
“Based on my investigation, Datuk Seri Azlan Man could not opt for cash payments for overseas leave facilities (Kuala Lumpur to London) and hotel accommodations for the years 2013 to 2017,” she said.
Responding to questions from Deputy Public Prosecutor Haresh Prakash Somiah regarding verification of the non-existent trips, the witness said the information was obtained from both travel agencies involved.
According to Theenmoly, the agencies reported that the accused cancelled the trips on confirmed dates under the pretext of managing the travel himself, while also requesting that the funds be deposited into his personal account.
“The travel agencies also confirmed that no tickets were purchased for Datuk Seri Azlan Man’s overseas vacation claims for the years 2014 to 2017,” said Theenmoly, who now serves as an investigating officer at the Ministry of Youth and Sports’ Integrity Unit.
When questioned by lawyer Burhanudeen Abdul Wahid, representing Azlan, about the need to investigate the accused’s passport, Theenmoly explained that there was no need to do so as the investigation was already solidified by leave application documents, local orders, price quotations, and invoices.
Azlan, 67, faces five charges related to false claims for overseas leave facilities totaling over RM1.18 million, allegedly committed at the Perlis State Secretariat Office between December 2013 and December 2017.
These claims involved flight ticket quotations to London, United Kingdom, as well as invoices from SKTT and Aidil Travel & Tours.
He is also facing five charges of receiving proceeds from illegal activities amounting to RM1.06 million between February 19, 2014, and December 2017 at various locations, including the Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman Maybank branch, CIMB Bank Putrajaya branch, and the Sheraton Imperial Hotel here.
Additionally, Azlan faces five alternative charges of misappropriating RM1.06 million belonging to the Perlis State Government that was paid to SKTT and Aidil Travel & Tours.
The trial before Judge Azura Alwi will resume on June 19. — Bernama

Title: IGP urges restraint over N. Sembilan political crisis, assures public order intact
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has urged for calm and restraint amid an ongoing political crisis in Negeri Sembilan, as law enforcement work to ensure public order.
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said what is currently taking place in Negeri Sembilan relates to customs practiced and the public should allow due process to proceed without interference.
“I am urging all parties to remain calm and refrain from making unnecessary comments.
“As a law enforcement agency, PDRM will always ensure the safety and harmony of the state remains intact,” he said during a press conference at the federal police headquarters in Bukit Aman here.
Mohd Khalid said numerous police reports have been lodged by relevant parties, mostly related to dissatisfaction, but did not provide their breakdown.
“We will conduct the necessary investigations on them,” he said.
The state’s administrative crisis was triggered when all 14 Umno and Barisan Nasional assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan unanimously declared a loss of confidence and withdrew their support for the state government led by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun following a leadership conflict.
However, Aminuddin was given the mandate to continue leading the state administration after receiving the consent of the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.

Title: Viral clip of ‘emaciated’ Malayan tiger in Japan sparks renewed concerns over welfare of Malaysian animals abroad (VIDEO)
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — A viral social media post showing a visibly underweight Malayan tiger in a Japanese zoo has stirred concern among Malaysians, intensifying debate over the welfare of Malaysian wildlife sent overseas.
The clip, circulating widely on Threads, showed the tiger pacing slowly inside its enclosure before sitting and lying down on a patch of grass, with its ribs and hip bones clearly visible.
Alongside the video, a user identified as gazelle.64528627 wrote: “Just look at the tigers in Japanese zoos…I can’t bear to watch. They really don’t know how to take care of animals. Is DAK going to end up like this too? So cruel, extremely cruel… I hope they get what they deserve!”
The post quickly went viral, with users sharing comparisons between animals in Japanese and Malaysian zoos and questioning welfare standards. Some described the tiger’s condition as distressing, while others speculated about illness or environmental stress factors.
The discussion has since expanded to the welfare of three Malaysian elephants — Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK) — currently housed at Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan.
According to a report published in
The Star
yesterday, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup has said the elephants should be brought back to Malaysia and relocated to the Taiping Zoo and Night Safari.
He said the proposal to repatriate the animals has gained public attention amid concerns over their condition, including claims that Japan’s climate may be unsuitable and reports alleging that Kelat had sustained injuries, based on a memorandum submitted to the ministry following a recent protest.
The memorandum was presented by about 20 individuals led by social activist Datuk Dr Kalai Vanar during a gathering outside the ministry’s office on April 24, calling for the elephants’ return.
https://www.threads.com/@gazelle.64528627/post/DXgyQOyCcFZ
Kurup said the ministry views the proposal as a “significant and reasonable” option in the public interest, and confirmed that the memorandum has been forwarded to the Taiping Municipal Council and Zoo Taiping & Night Safari for further action.
The issue has further fuelled online debate over animal welfare standards abroad, with many Malaysians questioning whether wildlife transfers are adequately monitored and whether the animals’ long-term wellbeing is being safeguarded.
Meanwhile, Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail, in a Facebook post yesterday evening, also urged Zoo Taiping & Night Safari to bring the elephants back to Malaysia and relocate them to the National Elephant Conservation Centre in Kuala Gandah, Temerloh.
Wan Rosdy said he was closely monitoring concerns raised by the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister, NGOs and members of the public, stressing that the animals form part of Malaysia’s biodiversity heritage.
He said the matter should not be taken lightly and called for the elephants’ immediate return, arguing that Kuala Gandah offers a more suitable environment for their long-term welfare, including space, social interaction and psychological well-being.
The three elephants were originally sent to Japan following discussions between Zoo Taiping & Night Safari, the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) and Tennoji Zoo that began in 2022.
Perak authorities had previously said the relocation formed part of a bilateral cooperation and animal exchange programme, formalised through a memorandum of understanding between the institutions involved.
State officials had also assured that the elephants’ welfare would be monitored, with zoo representatives expected to conduct follow-up visits to assess their condition after relocation.
However, public concern over the transfer had already emerged earlier, including an online petition opposing the move that attracted tens of thousands of signatures.

Title: Mubarak ordered to vacate Sungei Ujong Undang official residence after 30 years
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — Datuk Mubarak Dohak has been ordered to immediately vacate the official residence at Balai Luak Undang Sungei Ujong, where he has stayed for more than three decades.
A notice dated April 29 issued by the Negeri Sembilan State Secretary’s Office (SUK) began circulating on Thursday, according a report published by
Sinar Harian
today.
The letter, signed by Management Services Division secretary Akmal Farid Rizal Noordin, stated that the directive follows a Special Sitting of the Council of Justice and Custom (Dewan Keadilan dan Undang, DKU) held on April 17.
“In view of this, you are instructed to vacate the official residence and Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong with immediate effect. All state government assets registered at the premises must be kept in good condition for future inspection,” it said.
However, state secretary Datuk Mohd Zafir Ibrahim declined to confirm the eviction order when contacted.
Sources said state staff serving at the premises have also been instructed to be reassigned.
A check at the location showed no sign of eviction activity, and Mubarak is understood to still be residing there.
Previously, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said Mubarak’s actions in signing and reading out a declaration purportedly removing the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, could not be recognised.
He said Mubarak no longer had authority or function as Undang Luak Sungei Ujong, adding that the move was not in line with Articles 10 to 12 of the Negeri Sembilan Constitution.
